introduction: Discount is the reduction given on Marked Price (MP) to arrive at Selling Price (SP). It is closely linked with Profit-Loss (SP connects to CP) and Percentage. SSC CGL tests single discounts, successive discounts, discount schemes comparison, and buy-X-get-Y-free offers.

concepts.explanation: MP --discount--> SP --profit/loss--> CP. Discount is always calculated on Marked Price, never on Cost Price.
When two discounts x% and y% are applied one after another, Net discount% = x + y - xy/100 (note the MINUS sign, opposite to successive increase in percentage).
Effective discount% = [Y/(X+Y)] x 100. Customer pays for X items but takes (X+Y) items.

concepts.concept: MP hike then discount to reach original price
concepts.explanation: If MP raised by x%, discount needed to reverse = [x/(100+x)] x 100.
If sold at discount x% on MP, and CP is known as fraction of MP, profit/loss can be derived by comparing CP with SP=MP(100-x)/100.

formulas: Successive discounts x%, y%: Net% = x + y - xy/100
Three successive discounts: apply net% formula twice (combine first two, then combine with third)
Buy X get Y free: Discount% = Y/(X+Y) x 100
MP hiked by x% then discount needed to sell at original price = [x/(100+x)] x 100
If sold at x% discount, profit% = 5x/3 % relation: SP=MP(100-x)/100 = CP(100+5x/3)/100 (specific to certain markup relation, verify per question)
Comparing two discount schemes with equal sum: scheme with LARGER GAP between the two discount %s gives MORE total discount (better for customer, worse for shopkeeper)

question_types.identification_method: Direct question giving any two of MP, SP, discount% and asking the third.
question_types.when_to_use: Use direct formula SP=MP(100-d)/100 or discount%=(MP-SP)/MP x100.
step_by_step: Identify which two values are given (MP, SP, or discount%)
Apply the relevant formula to find the missing value
Convert % to fraction for faster calculation if numbers are clean
question_types.shortcut: Use fraction form of discount% (e.g. 37.5%=3/8) rather than decimal multiplication.
common_mistakes: Calculating discount on CP instead of MP
Sign error between increase(MP) and decrease(discount)

practice_questions.q: MP=₹10,490 sold for ₹9,441. Find discount%.
MP=₹550, discount=40%. Find SP.
MP=₹300, discount=37.5%. Find SP.
An article's MP is ₹200, sold at 30% discount. Find SP.
MP=₹8400, CP=₹4000, sold at 25% discount then extra 15% on discounted price. Find profit%.
practice_questions.solution: SP=8400x3/4x17/20=₹4335;approx per notes=134% of CP base(100:134)=>34% profit

question_types.identification_method: Question mentions two or more discounts applied one after another (e.g. '20% and then 25%').
question_types.when_to_use: Use net% formula x+y-xy/100 repeatedly (combine 2 at a time) OR multiply fractions directly: MP x (100-x)/100 x (100-y)/100...
step_by_step: List all discount percentages in order
Combine first two using net% = x+y-xy/100
Combine result with third discount using same formula again
Final net% gives overall single equivalent discount
question_types.shortcut: For 3 equal discounts of d% each: combine first two, then combine result with third — do NOT just add all three.
common_mistakes: Simply adding all discount percentages (20%+25%=45% is WRONG, correct is 40%)
Forgetting to combine step-wise for 3+ discounts

practice_questions.q: Successive discounts of 20% and 25%. Find net discount.
Successive discounts 5%,10%,20%. Find net discount.
Three successive discounts of 10% each. Find net discount.
MP=₹1000. Discounts 25%&5% vs 20%&10%. Find SP difference.
Difference between single 65% discount and successive 30%&40% discount is ₹105. Find MP.

question_types.type_name: Buy X Get Y Free (Freebie Schemes)
question_types.identification_method: Phrases like 'Buy 5 get 3 free', 'Buy 4 get 1 free'.
question_types.when_to_use: Use effective discount% = Y/(X+Y) x 100 directly instead of computing per-item cost.
step_by_step: Identify X (paid items) and Y (free items)
Total items received = X+Y
Effective discount% = Y/(X+Y) x 100
question_types.shortcut: Buy 5 get 3 free = 3/8=37.5% | Buy 4 get 1 free=1/5=20% | Buy 6 get 10=4/10=40%.
common_mistakes: Confusing 'buy X get Y' (total Y items) with 'buy X get Y free' (X+Y total items)

practice_questions.q: Buy 5 get 3 free. Find discount%.
Buy 6 and get 10 (total). Find discount%.
Buy 4 get 1 free, combined with 5% extra discount on bill >15000. If payable before 5% discount is 16000, find effective total discount.

question_types.identification_method: Question gives 2-4 different discount schemes and asks which gives min/max discount or best for customer/shopkeeper.
question_types.when_to_use: When sum of two discounts is EQUAL across schemes, compare the GAP between the two %s: bigger gap = bigger net discount.
step_by_step: Calculate net discount% for each scheme using successive discount formula (or Y/(X+Y) for freebie schemes)
If sums are equal, just compare gap between the two discount values (no need for full calculation)
Rank schemes: highest discount% = best for customer/worst for shopkeeper (lowest SP)
Pick the scheme matching what's asked (min discount, max discount, lowest SP, etc.)
question_types.shortcut: Equal sum shortcut: Scheme with 0% gap (equal discounts, e.g. 8%&8%) gives LEAST discount; scheme with biggest gap (e.g. 16%&0%) gives MOST discount.
common_mistakes: Calculating full successive % for every scheme when shortcut (gap comparison) would save time
Confusing 'minimum discount = best for shopkeeper' with 'best for customer'

practice_questions.q: Schemes: (1) 5%&5% (2) single 10% (3) 8%&2%. Which gives minimum discount?
Schemes: (A) 24%&27% successive (B) buy5get3free (C) buy6get10. Which gives minimum discount?
Schemes: (i)10%&8% (ii)12%&10% (iii)12%&12% (iv)20% flat. Which gives lowest SP?
A:8%&8%, B:11%&5%, C:13%&3%, D:16%flat. Minimum price scheme?

question_types.type_name: MP Hike then Discount to Restore Original Price
question_types.identification_method: Shopkeeper raises MP by x%, then question asks discount% needed to sell at ORIGINAL price.
step_by_step: Note the % hike x in MP
New MP = Old MP x (100+x)/100
Discount needed = (New MP - Old MP)/New MP x 100 = x/(100+x) x100
question_types.shortcut: Memorize: hike x% then discount x/(100+x)x100 restores original — mirror of profit-on-SP concept.
common_mistakes: Using x% itself as the discount answer instead of x/(100+x)x100
Forgetting new MP is the base for discount%, not old MP

practice_questions.q: MP raised by 40%. What discount% restores original price?

question_types.identification_method: Question gives relation between CP and MP (e.g. MP is x% above CP) plus a discount, and asks profit% or vice versa.
question_types.when_to_use: Build a single CP-MP-SP chain: CP -> MP (markup) -> SP (discount), then compare CP directly with SP.
step_by_step: Set CP=100 (or given value)
Find MP using markup% given
Apply discount% on MP to get SP
Compare SP with CP to get profit/loss%
question_types.shortcut: Chain multiplication: SP = CP x (100+markup)/100 x (100-discount)/100 — compute directly in one line.
common_mistakes: Applying discount on CP instead of MP
Not setting CP=100 which simplifies calculation significantly

practice_questions.q: MP is 50% more than CP. Successive discounts 5%,10%,20% given. Find profit%.
Sold at 5% discount, profit=14%. If discount=11%, find new profit%.
CP=₹4000, MP=₹8400, discount 25% then extra 15%. Find profit%.
Retailer allows 25% trade discount + 8% extra, profit=15% on cost. Find markup% on CP.
Shopkeeper marks k% above CP, gives 20% discount, gains 25%. Find k.

identification_tricks: Only MP, SP, discount% mentioned (no CP) — Basic Discount type.
Two/three % discounts applied in sequence — Successive Discount, use x+y-xy/100.
'Buy X get Y free' wording — Freebie scheme, use Y/(X+Y)x100.
Multiple discount schemes compared — check if sums are equal, use GAP method.
MP raised first then discount to reach original price — use x/(100+x)x100.
CP, MP, and discount% all mentioned together with profit/loss asked — combined CP-MP-SP chain type.
solving_approach: Step 1: Identify all three price stages present in question — CP, MP, SP.
Step 2: Note that discount always acts on MP to give SP; profit/loss always compares CP with SP.
Step 3: Use ratio/fraction method for one-step discount; use successive % formula for multi-step discounts.
Step 4: For freebie schemes, immediately convert to Y/(X+Y) fraction — do not calculate item-by-item.
Step 5: When comparing schemes, check equal-sum shortcut before doing full calculations.
Step 6: Cross-verify final answer using CP:SP or MP:SP ratio matching given options.
shortcuts: Successive discount formula has MINUS sign (x+y-xy/100), successive increase has PLUS — do not confuose with percentage increase topic.
Buy X get Y free ALWAYS equals Y/(X+Y) x 100 — instant formula, no need to calculate per unit price.
Equal-sum discount schemes: bigger gap between two %s = bigger overall discount.
Three successive equal discounts of d%: combine stepwise, never simply multiply d x 3.
MP hike x% reversed by discount x/(100+x)x100 — same pattern as 'profit on SP' conversion in Profit-Loss.
common_mistakes: Adding successive discount percentages directly (20%+25%≠45%, actual=40%).
Applying discount on CP instead of MP.
Using X/(X+Y) instead of Y/(X+Y) in freebie scheme discount%.
Forgetting new MP (after hike) becomes the base for reverse discount%, not old MP.
Not combining three successive discounts stepwise (combining all at once incorrectly).
pyq_patterns: SSC CGL loves comparing 3-4 discount schemes (successive vs freebie vs flat) in a single question testing all shortcuts together.
Successive discount + profit% combined questions are very common, testing CP-MP-SP chain understanding.
'Buy X get Y free' is almost always tested alongside a plain % discount for comparison.
Reverse questions (discount% needed to restore price after MP hike) appear regularly in Tier-1 and Tier-2.
quick_revision: Buy X get Y free: discount%=Y/(X+Y)x100
MP hike x% reversed by discount x/(100+x)x100
Equal sum schemes: bigger gap = bigger discount
Discount always on MP; Profit/Loss always CP vs SP
exam_tips: Never add successive discount percentages directly — always use the formula with minus xy/100 term.
For scheme comparison questions, check equal-sum shortcut first to save 30+ seconds.
Draw a simple CP->MP->SP flow chart on rough sheet for combined discount-profit questions.
Memorize common freebie discount% values: buy5get3free=37.5%, buy4get1free=20%, buy6get10=40%.
